content.theorem: For any convergent iteration, T-minus (iterations remaining to convergence) can be estimated from the current residual and the convergence rate
content.proof: If convergence rate is r (e.g., linear convergence: |x_{n+1} - x*| ≈ r|x_n - x*|), then iterations to reach tolerance ε: n ≈ log(|x_0 - x*| / ε) / log(1/r). This gives T-minus: the number of iterations remaining. The clock on the wall shows this countdown in real time.
content.benchmark: Newton's method (quadratic): T-minus typically < 10 iterations. Gradient descent (linear): T-minus depends on condition number. Batch snap: T-minus = 1 (single pass).
content.caveat: Estimated T-minus assumes smooth convergence. Oscillatory or chaotic convergence invalidates the estimate. Monitor actual residual, not just the countdown.
